title: Black Moon
wikitext:
[]: Black Moon             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 17, 2008 11:22 pm I tried to search to see if this was done before and couldn't find anything relevant. I came across information that the Black Moon MAY have been based off of. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lilith_%28hypothetical_moon%29 "Lilith is a hypothetical natural satellite of Earth whose existence is not supported by any scientific evidence. In 1918 an astrologer Sepharial named the hypothetical moon Lilith and opined that it was dark enough to have escaped detection. The name Lilith is of near-eastern mythology, and (according to post-Biblical Jewish lore) was the first wife to Adam. The moon Lilith is sometimes referred to as the "Dark Moon," "Black Moon," or "Ghost Moon". Lilith was incorporated into horoscopes and some astrologers use it in their calculations to this day." It's also apparently treated in hypothetical astrology as representing the "unnatural" or "the intrusive", which reflects the Second Angel's nature to the planet quite well. Relevant? Coincidence? Anno actually DOING TEH RESARCH? You decide.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Sep 17, 2008 11:28 pm Of course there was research in the development of the series, geez. Otherwise we wouldn't have found all the connections betwixt the series and other real world religious stuff. Very relevant. But that is very, very interesting, though.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 18, 2008 2:06 am Somebody didn't get the old joke.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Sep 18, 2008 9:54 am Someone probably wasn't here for the joke... Anyway, I'd wager relevant, and Anno spent like what, two years working on the outline of the series? He probably traveled to the dark corners of the world, seeing a lot of this stuff first-hand.

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Thu Sep 18, 2008 5:29 pm I particularly like this bit: In 1926, the Science journal Die Sterne published the findings of an amateur German astronomer named W. Spill, who claimed to have successfully viewed a second moon orbiting the earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Sep 19, 2008 9:51 am Existing thread on this and Evapedia article .

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Sep 19, 2008 11:10 am Die Sterne? Quite a coincidence, indeed! Zuggy's first post in that thread and the page linked to it contain some real heavy astrology info, so be warned.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 19, 2008 12:04 pm Zuggy wrote: Existing thread on this and Evapedia article .

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Sep 20, 2008 3:21 pm Well, it was Zuggy, so I'm not surprised.
